name: Red Hill Truffles
signature: Peninsula truffière running seasonal truffle hunts with trained dogs — a morning in the field, a cellar-door tasting, and fresh truffles for sale when in season
editorNote:
Red Hill Truffles is a working truffière on the ridge that opens for seasonal truffle hunts from late autumn through winter. The format is the one every winter food-obsessive on the Peninsula eventually books: a morning in the field with the trained dogs, watching them work each row and nose out the black truffle hidden under the inoculated oaks, followed by a small tasting back at the shed where the truffles go into eggs, butter, and whatever the kitchen is running that week.

Truffle seasons are short and the weekend slots book out early every year. Go in June or July if you can, dress warmly, and understand that the point is the process — the hunt itself — as much as the truffle on the plate. Fresh truffles are available for sale when they are up.

One of the Peninsula's most unusual winter experiences. Book months in advance for weekends.
